title: ranunculus constantinopolitanus var. villosus
intro: Ranunculus constantinopolitanus var. villosus, a lesser-known variant of the buttercup plant, is a species of flowering plant native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. This plant, also known as hairy lesser celandine, has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus constantinopolitanus var. villosus include its potential to treat skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and wounds, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
